variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_topo/topo.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case starts with a line 'N M', then M lines of 'u v' meaning u must come before v in the order (u and v are integers in 0..N-1). For each case, output either a valid topological ordering of all N nodes (space-separated on one line) or the literal string 'CYCLE' if the graph has a cycle. Use Kahn's algorithm.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_topo; python3 topo.py < input.txt > out.txt; python3 check.py

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_topo/topo.go (package main). Reads stdin: T cases, each 'N M' then M edges 'u v'. Output topological order of 0..N-1 (space-separated) or 'CYCLE'. Use Kahn's algorithm. Compile with `go build -o topo topo.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_topo; go build -o topo topo.go; ./topo < input.txt > out.txt; python3 check.py

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_topo/topo.c — reads stdin: T cases, each 'N M' then M edges 'u v'. Output topological order (space-separated) or 'CYCLE'. Use Kahn's algorithm. N <= 1000 per case is sufficient. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o topo topo.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_topo; gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o topo topo.c; ./topo < input.txt > out.txt; python3 check.py

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_topo/topo.cpp — reads stdin: T cases, each 'N M' then M edges 'u v'. Output topological order or 'CYCLE'. Use Kahn's algorithm. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o topo topo.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_topo; g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o topo topo.cpp; ./topo < input.txt > out.txt; python3 check.py

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_topo/topo.rs — same I/O contract as the other variants (see Python/Go/C/C++ siblings). Idiomatic Rust 2021+. Will be compiled with `rustc -O topo.rs -o topo` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_topo && rustc -O topo.rs -o topo && ./topo < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_topo/topo.zig — same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`, `std.Io.File.stdin()` / `stdout()` for I/O, `takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns optional, null at EOF) or `appendRemainingUnlimited` to slurp stdin. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast topo.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_topo && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast topo.zig && ./topo < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py
